# Support

> Need a hand on the night shift? Here's where to knock.

The 5 to 9 is early, experimental software maintained by one person on
nights and weekends. Routing your ask to the right place gets you a faster answer.

## I have a question or an idea

Start a **[GitHub Discussion](https://github.com/jakecastillo/the-5-to-9/discussions)**.
How-do-I questions, "is this a bug or am I holding it wrong," shift-report show-and-tell,
and feature brainstorming all live there. Search first — someone may have asked already.

## I found a bug

Open a **[bug report](https://github.com/jakecastillo/the-5-to-9/issues/new?template=bug_report.yml)**.
Include your OS/shell (Git Bash?), Claude Code version, the command or engine
(`/clock-in` vs. `scripts/night-shift.sh`), and the actual vs. expected behavior.
Scrub secrets out of any logs — the crew runs under bypass-permissions, so logs can
be spicy.

## I want to propose a feature

Open a **[feature request](https://github.com/jakecastillo/the-5-to-9/issues/new?template=feature_request.yml)**,
or float it in Discussions first if it's still half-baked.

## I found a security issue

**Do not open a public issue.** Follow [SECURITY.md](../SECURITY.md) — private report
via GitHub Security Advisories.

## Before you trust it with a real repo

Read the [README](../README.md) and the [Safety](../README.md#safety) section. This is
a new hire on the night shift: capable, worth watching. Start small, cap the loop,
review the shift branch.

---

_Response times are best-effort — it's a night shift, after all. Keep it kind
(see the [Code of Conduct](../CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md))._
